operation: First, a CadQuery workplane is initialized on the XZ plane. A 2D sketch is started on this workplane: a centered ellipse with a 6 unit horizontal radius and 4 unit vertical radius is drawn first, followed by a centered rectangle with 12 units of width and 6 units of height, producing a 2D profile corresponding to the intersection of these two shapes. The sketch is finalized, then the closed 2D profile is extruded 4 units along the Y-axis to create the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a uniform prismatic solid with a constant cross-section across its full depth. Its two end faces are identical flat planar shapes perpendicular to the depth axis, formed by intersecting an ellipse and a rectangle: the cross-section has straight parallel vertical end edges, straight parallel horizontal top and bottom edges, and smooth curved arcs connecting the ends of the horizontal and vertical edges. All lateral faces along the depth are either flat planar faces or smooth curved faces.
function: This compact prismatic component is designed to be used as a spacer, structural insert, or custom profile element in mechanical assemblies, featuring flat surfaces for stable mounting and curved edges that reduce stress concentrations and support compatibility with curved housing designs.
